summary: Stunning soundboard capture of a Jerry‑led winter ’79 set featuring a smoking He's Gone, epic The Other One and arguably the best Shakedown ever heard.
review: All five reviewers rave about this show’s energy and improvisational peaks – from a blistering He’s Gone jam to an inspired rendition of The Other One and a standout Shakedown. The Miller‑sourced soundboard tape is praised as clear and well balanced, with only a note that the recording is slightly incomplete, but overall it delivers an excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Uniform five‑star reviews highlight exceptional musical performance and a high‑quality soundboard source; only minor comment about incompleteness keeps the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard (SBD) recording described as clear, well‑balanced and very listenable; one reviewer notes the set is not fully complete but this does not detract from overall quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A Jerry‑driven, high‑energy flow with long, mellow jams; the band rides a cohesive groove throughout, even without a traditional Space segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise his tone, an extra‑long solo, and his vocal croon on Black Peter – a true centerpiece of the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for briefly reverting to a pre‑Brent chorus/verse arrangement during a song, an interesting spontaneous moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: References to the pre‑Brent keyboard style (Keith era) and a brief comment that Hart sits in sounding like Garcia and Brent, indicating solid keyboard work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumz segment highlighted as “choice”; the percussion is singled out for its quality.
